title: Shareholder Meeting
weakest_section: 13:00-14:00 range with forced Southern accent explanations

jokes.text: You cannot go wrong with a throat punch.
jokes.setup: Dwight teaching martial arts with an elaborate yakuza/triad scenario
jokes.punchline: Simplifies the complex scenario to just throat punching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - overcomplicates then oversimplifies. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Last time, you pulled my pants down, and then you tried to choke me with my shoelace.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking Kevin to attack him for a demonstration
jokes.punchline: Kevin's specific, embarrassing memory of previous encounter
jokes.explanation: Great specificity and builds Dwight's history of inappropriate training methods.

jokes.text: False. I did choke you with your shoelace.
jokes.setup: Kevin saying Dwight 'tried' to choke him
jokes.punchline: Dwight correcting that he succeeded
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Dwight - pedantic correction that makes him look worse. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: I think the most worthy opponent of you is you.
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting who should fight Dwight
jokes.punchline: Implies Dwight is his own worst enemy
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim setup for Dwight's absurdity. Good character work but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Dwight fighting himself with elaborate self-narration
jokes.setup: Dwight agreeing to demonstrate fighting himself
jokes.punchline: Extended sequence of Dwight literally fighting himself with commentary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy concept perfectly suited to Dwight's character. Loses points for length.

jokes.text: Oh, my god, he's making you look like such a fool.
jokes.setup: Dwight fighting himself
jokes.punchline: Jim commenting as if there are two different people
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim moment - plays along with the absurdity while highlighting how ridiculous it is.

jokes.text: You two are so evenly matched, I don't know how one of you's gonna get the upper hand.
jokes.setup: Continuation of treating Dwight vs himself as two people
jokes.punchline: Commenting on the 'close competition' of one person
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke but feels slightly repetitive.

jokes.text: I get like eight emails a day.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking about a specific email
jokes.punchline: Michael's low email volume reveals his irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Michael's delusion about his importance.

jokes.text: Your feelings journal? You told me to put it in the time capsule.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Erin about 'the journal'
jokes.punchline: Erin assumes he means his feelings journal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals both characters - Michael's emotional needs, Erin's literalness.

jokes.text: It's $1.99 to finish the article.
jokes.setup: Article cuts off mid-sentence about potential bankruptcy
jokes.punchline: Paywall at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Great situational comedy - modern paywall frustration at the worst moment.

jokes.text: You can all have jobs at Schrute Farms... as human scarecrows.
jokes.setup: Everyone worried about losing jobs
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre job offer with terrible conditions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - tries to be helpful but makes bizarre, unhelpful offer. Great callback integration.

jokes.text: It doesn't pay much and you can't unionize.
jokes.setup: Offering jobs as human scarecrows
jokes.punchline: Making the already bad offer worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Dwight's anti-union stance. Solid follow-up.

jokes.text: I'll catch you on the flippity-flip.
jokes.setup: Michael ending a phone conversation
jokes.punchline: His made-up, unprofessional sign-off
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael - trying to sound cool but being ridiculous. Very quotable.

jokes.text: No, just poopin'. You know how I be.
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Michael threw up in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Michael's overly casual, inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate TMI response with fake casual tone. Good cringe.

jokes.text: Crazy world, lotta smells.
jokes.setup: Explaining the throw-up smell
jokes.punchline: Philosophical deflection about smells
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and absurd wisdom. Perfect Michael deflection. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It is time for the nard-dog to take matters into his own paws.
jokes.setup: Andy deciding to ask Erin out
jokes.punchline: Mixing his dog nickname metaphor with actual dog anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Andy's ridiculous self-nicknames and mixed metaphors.

jokes.text: Monkey pee all over you.
jokes.setup: Jim saying 'monkey see, monkey do'
jokes.punchline: Michael's crude rhyming addition
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor that fits Michael's immaturity but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: There has been a murder!
jokes.setup: Everyone expecting news about the company
jokes.punchline: Michael dramatically announcing a fake murder
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and dramatic delivery. Classic Michael deflection from real problems.

jokes.text: There's been a murder... in Savannah.
jokes.setup: Building tension with murder announcement
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's just a game
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the misdirection, though less impactful than the setup.

jokes.text: Operation got me through my vasectomy, i.e. my operation.
jokes.setup: Listing games that got him through tough times
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily explaining the obvious connection
jokes.explanation: Michael over-explaining an obvious joke, which is itself the joke. Decent character work.

jokes.text: Tube city. You owe me one.
jokes.setup: Jim saying this is a terrible idea
jokes.punchline: Michael referencing a previous ridiculous idea Jim stopped
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect economy. The absurdity is in the name alone.

jokes.text: Jim's talking head about stopping Tube City
jokes.setup: Jim explaining co-managing compromises
jokes.punchline: Revealing the ridiculous hamster tube city plan
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the Tube City callback. The visual absurdity is brilliant.

jokes.text: If you talk slowly in real life, your character could, say, have been kicked in the head by a horse.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining how to create characters
jokes.punchline: Suggesting a mean-spirited backstory for Kevin
jokes.explanation: Michael being insensitive to Kevin while trying to be helpful. Typical character move.

jokes.text: Cool. I'll try it.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Kevin was kicked by a horse
jokes.punchline: Kevin's oblivious, enthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin response - misses the insult and goes along enthusiastically.

jokes.text: Nellie's brother.
jokes.setup: Andy and Erin discovering they're assigned as siblings
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable realization after romantic tension
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment setting up their romantic subplot complications.

jokes.text: Oh! I make them feel beautiful.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his playboy character's activities
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly sweet/pathetic take on being a playboy
jokes.explanation: Michael's version of being a playboy is making women feel good about themselves. Sweet and sad.

jokes.text: You don't have to keep saying 'I do declare.' Anytime you say something it means you're declaring.
jokes.setup: Michael overusing 'I do declare'
jokes.punchline: Jim's logical explanation of why it's redundant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim - logical takedown of Michael's affectation with smart observation.

jokes.text: Delta Burke, I do declare.
jokes.setup: Jim asking what southern woman he's basing his accent on
jokes.punchline: Random Delta Burke reference while immediately repeating the phrase
jokes.explanation: Michael ignoring Jim's advice and making obscure reference. Typical behavior.

jokes.text: That's clever. Debutante.
jokes.setup: Pam reading her character name 'Deborah Utante'
jokes.punchline: Michael proudly pointing out the obvious pun
jokes.explanation: Michael being proud of catching an obvious pun. Good character moment.

jokes.text: You got this kind of like Florida panhandle thing goin', whereas what you really want is more of a Savannah accent, which is more like molasses, just sort of spillin' out of your mouth.
jokes.setup: Michael correcting Pam's southern accent
jokes.punchline: Overly specific accent coaching with flowery description
jokes.explanation: Michael getting way too into accent coaching. The molasses description is nicely specific.

jokes.text: Oh, now do the Swedish Chef.
jokes.setup: Michael doing accent work
jokes.punchline: Creed's random request for Muppet character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed - completely random request that derails the scene. Great economy.

jokes.text: He lives on Sesame Street, dumbass.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what province Swedish Chef is from
jokes.punchline: Creed's wrong correction with insult
jokes.explanation: Creed being confidently wrong and insulting. Classic character move.

jokes.text: Sir, there has been a murder, and you are a suspect.
jokes.setup: Darryl arriving late asking what's going on
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately making him a suspect
jokes.explanation: Michael's commitment to the game and immediate inclusion of newcomers. Good character work.

jokes.text: It's not my fault. I was exposed to Harry Potter.
jokes.setup: Playing a voodoo character
jokes.punchline: Blaming Harry Potter for his dark arts involvement
jokes.explanation: Funny excuse that treats Harry Potter like a gateway drug. Good contemporary reference.

jokes.text: It's never the person you most suspect. It's also never the person you least suspect, since anyone with half a brain would suspect them the most.
jokes.setup: Trying to solve the murder mystery
jokes.punchline: Dwight's convoluted logic about suspects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic that's actually fairly sound but overly complicated. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Therefore, I know the killer to be Phyllis, a.k.a. Beatrix Bourbon, the person I most medium suspect.
jokes.setup: His complex suspect logic
jokes.punchline: Concluding with 'medium suspect' and random accusation
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the suspect logic with 'medium suspect' - perfectly Dwight conclusion.

jokes.text: Well, you know old Nellie is always up for a romp in the hay.
jokes.setup: Andy asking her out in character
jokes.punchline: Erin's overly sexual response as Nellie
jokes.explanation: Erin being unexpectedly forward in character, creating awkwardness for Andy.

jokes.text: I'm a little worried I might have asked out Naughty Nellie and not Erin.
jokes.setup: Erin's sexual response to his date request
jokes.punchline: Andy realizing he might have asked out the wrong 'person'
jokes.explanation: Good recognition of the character/reality confusion. Andy's genuine concern is funny.

jokes.text: This plantation, we're runnin' low on, uh, greenbacks, and we're havin' problems payin' the people who give us the seeds and the dirt.
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to explain financial problems in character
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex business to seeds and dirt
jokes.explanation: Oscar trying to stay in character while explaining serious business issues. Good juxtaposition.

jokes.text: That is a mis-- that's a misdirection.
jokes.setup: Game reveals Phyllis was the killer
jokes.punchline: Michael refusing to accept the game's conclusion
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to let the game end naturally. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Of course, Nathaniel.
jokes.setup: Andy asking if she's excited about their date
jokes.punchline: Still responding as character instead of herself
jokes.explanation: Continuing the character/reality confusion. Sets up the awkward realization.

jokes.text: Savannah? That's a far way from Scranton.
jokes.setup: Andy mentioning Savannah steakhouse
jokes.punchline: Erin thinking he means the actual city
jokes.explanation: Perfect miscommunication moment. Erin's literalness creates the awkwardness.

jokes.text: I thought it was for real... So I was excited, but it was just-- it was part of the game.
jokes.setup: Confusion about whether the date was real
jokes.punchline: Erin's disappointed realization
jokes.explanation: Sweet and sad moment. Good character development for both Erin and Andy.

jokes.text: I think Michael may have snapped. Or maybe he's just stuck in character.
jokes.setup: Michael's increasingly erratic behavior
jokes.punchline: Jim's analysis of the two bad options
jokes.explanation: Good Jim observation about Michael's state. Both options are equally concerning.

jokes.text: Both. They're both worse.
jokes.setup: Jim asking which is worse - snapped or stuck
jokes.punchline: Pam's logical but unhelpful answer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Pam's practical but pessimistic response.

jokes.text: I'm the dead body, and these are my brain chunks.
jokes.setup: Michael lying on the floor with props
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of his 'corpse' setup
jokes.explanation: Michael's commitment to the game has reached absurd levels. Good visual comedy.

jokes.text: Let us have this stupid little game, all right?
jokes.setup: Jim trying to get Michael to take David's call
jokes.punchline: Michael's desperate plea while calling his own game stupid
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Michael knows it's stupid but needs the distraction. Very human.

jokes.text: We fully expect to be out of money by the end of the year-- insolvent.
jokes.setup: The company's financial situation
jokes.punchline: Stark contrast to the silly game happening
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from the juxtaposition of real financial doom with the murder game.

jokes.text: There has been another murder.
jokes.setup: Jim asking for news about the company
jokes.punchline: Michael continuing to deflect with game announcements
jokes.explanation: Michael's continued avoidance, but the joke is getting repetitive by now.

jokes.text: Jim's talking head about co-managing and the lifeboat analogy
jokes.setup: Reflecting on the day's events
jokes.punchline: Comparing Michael's game to a parent distracting kids on a lifeboat
jokes.explanation: Beautiful metaphor that reframes Michael's behavior as protective rather than just avoidant. Excellent insight.

jokes.text: Today is the hardest I have worked in a long, long time.
jokes.setup: Playing the murder mystery game all day
jokes.punchline: Stanley considering acting like the hardest work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley - lazy but engaged when something interests him. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Andy revealed himself to be a double agent, at which point, Dwight felt comfortable revealing that he also was a double agent, and then, Michael announced to everybody that, get this, he was a double agent.
jokes.setup: The game's climactic reveals
jokes.punchline: Everyone being double agents defeats the purpose
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the game devolved into meaninglessness. Everyone wants to be special.

jokes.text: Get the keys out of my purse, start the car.
jokes.setup: Game getting too intense
jokes.punchline: Phyllis ready to flee like it's real
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Phyllis treating the game like real danger. Nice character moment.

tmdb_overview: Michael speaks out of line at the Dunder Mifflin shareholders meeting and creates a big problem for management, while Jim discovers that nobody in the office thinks he's the "real" boss.
